content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                322-5901                    322-6441


December 6, 1976


James E. Satt, Judge
Municipal Court
Los Angeles Judicial District
County Courthouse
Los Angeles, CA    90012

Dear Judge Satt:

     This letter is in response to your letter of November 24, 1976, regarding the filing of campaign statements. Please be advised that filing campaign statements with the Registrar of Voters instead of the county clerk, is insufficient to comply with the requirements of the Political Reform Act, if the county clerk deems the Registrar of Voters to be the filing officer of the statements. Local officials may designate certain officials, such as the Registrar of Voters or the election officials, to be the filing officer of statements filed at the local levels in order to allow convenient maintenance of statements in offices which handle similar matters.

     If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                                        Sincerely,

                                                        [illegible]
                                                        Dawn Wiser
                                                        Technical Assistance and
                                                        Analysis Division

IN CHAMBERS

MUNICIPAL COURT
LOS ANGELES JUDICIAL DISTRICT
JAMES E. SATT, JUDGE

November 24, 1976


State of California Fair Political
Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Sirs:

In reference to your letter of November 10, 1976,
regarding filing requirements for judges under the Political
Reform Act, I attempted to file an original and one copy of the candidate's campaign statement
form 430 with the County Clerk.

I was advised that the County Clerk in Los Angeles
County is not accepting these documents for filing, and
that the original and one copy filed with the Registrar
of Voters is sufficient to comply with your letter.

Will you please advise me if this ruling is correct
and if it is acceptable to you.

Very truly yours,

James E. Satt, Judge

JES:gg

sections.question_synthetic: Judge James E. Satt seeks clarification regarding whether the Los Angeles County Clerk's refusal to file candidate campaign statement documents complies with FPPC filing requirements, and whether filing one copy with the Registrar of Voters satisfies the Political Reform Act requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter FROM a judge TO the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document is incomplete/damaged with missing text sections. The judge references a prior FPPC letter dated November 10, 1976, but this response letter is not included. No formal Question/Conclusion sections found; extracted question from letter body. Document partially illegible in places.

Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Dear Sirs: In reference to your letter of November 10, 1976, regarding filing requirements for judges under the Political Reform Act, I attempted to file an original and one copy of the candidate's campaign statement form 430 with the County Clerk. embedding.summary: Judge James E. Satt writes to the FPPC seeking confirmation that the Los Angeles County Clerk correctly refused to file candidate campaign statement documents and asking whether filing with the Registrar of Voters instead complies with the Political Reform Act.
